title: athyrium niponicum var. pachyphlebium
intro: Athyrium niponicum var. pachyphlebium, commonly known as Japanese painted fern, is a type of flowering plant in the Athyrium genus and family Woodsiaceae. It is native to Japan and is known for its distinctive, bi-colored fronds that add a unique touch to gardens and landscapes. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and its therapeutic actions include calming and soothing effects on the nervous system. The bioactive constituents of Japanese painted fern include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

This plant also possesses antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote wound healing, and its adaptogenic properties can aid in stress reduction and improve overall mental health. The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids, like ferulic acid. The roots and rhizome are rich in saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. side_effects: Athyrium niponicum var. pachyphlebium can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, or gastrointestinal problems in some individuals. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach pain.
